figures.caption: Fig. 1: The reconstructed results of our proposed GIFD on ImageNet [11] and FFHQ [12]. The first column contains the randomly initialized images generated by GAN models. The next two columns show the reconstruction samples of the latent space search and our proposed GIFD.
Fig. 9: PSNR results of different methods for different global models on the ImageNet dataset.
TABLE VII: Ablation study of GIFD and its three variants on every 1000th image of ImageNet and FFHQ validation set.
Fig. 10: The cost function over time of GIAS and GIFD with B = 4. We give 4 trials and calculate the average values. Both methods execute a total of 8000 iterations.

Fig. 7: PSNR results of different attacks when changing the values of q (the probability of assigning a training example with a certain label to a particular client) on ImageNet. These settings strictly align with the previous works [52], [53] considering data heterogeneity.
TABLE V: PSNR mean of different attack methods under different batch sizes.
TABLE II: Comparison of GIFD with state-of-the-art baselines on OOD data of different styles.
TABLE IV: PSNR mean of different attack methods under different defense strategies.
